# Run 2026-07-01 (workflow run 28495024316)

Selected tasks: 5, 8, 2 + 11.

**Task 5 (Coding Improvements)** — Addressed auto-generated issue #163 (`workerLanguageBase` normalisation pair duplicated across 8 call sites). Branch `repo-assist/refactor-worker-language-pair-2026-07-01` (commit `7204370`):
- New helper `resolveWorkerLanguagePair({required sourceLanguage, required targetLanguage})` in `lib/features/ai/domain/worker_language_pair.dart` returning a `WorkerLanguagePair` record (`({String source, String target})`).
- 8 unit tests in `test/features/ai/domain/worker_language_pair_test.dart` covering BCP-47, underscore-separated tags, ISO 639-2 legacy aliases (`kor`/`zho`), empty/whitespace/null fallback to `'en'`, trim semantics, and round-trip equivalence with `workerLanguageBase`.
- Refactored 7 call sites: `byok_translation_capability.dart`, `enjoy_translation_capability.dart`, `byok_dictionary_capability.dart`, `enjoy_dictionary_capability.dart`, `translation_prompt.dart`, `dictionary_prompt.dart`, `enjoy_contextual_translation_capability.dart`. The contextual one also drops a dead-code `assert(() { workerLanguageBase(sourceLanguage); return true; }())` that discarded the source normalisation and only fired in debug.
- The prompts (`translation_prompt.dart`, `dictionary_prompt.dart`) now use the helper internally — public API stable, duplication removed.

**Task 8 (Performance Improvements)** — Addressed #151 backlog item #2 (artwork palette cache staleness). Branch `repo-assist/perf-palette-cache-invalidation-2026-07-01` (commit `6774b09`):
- LRU cache keyed on `path:size:mtime` instead of just `path`. Re-thumbnail flows that overwrite the file in place invalidate the entry on the next lookup instead of returning a stale palette for up to 32 lookups.
- Stats the file once via `File.statSync()` and reuses the result for both cache hit-check and insert — one syscall per lookup, no extra cost on the happy path.
- New `debugResetArtworkPaletteCache()` test hook.
- 4 unit tests in `test/core/theme/dynamic_color/artwork_palette_test.dart`: null/empty path, missing file, key includes size+mtime (not just path), overwriting the file with new bytes bumps the key.

**Task 2 (Issue Investigation and Comment)** — Engaged substantively on #163 (workerLanguageBase duplication) and #151 (palette cache staleness) — both issues this run's work addressed. Comments detail branch, verification, and the manual push/PR recipe (Actions cannot push in this repo).

**Task 11 (Monthly Activity Summary)** — Posted final June wrap-up comment on #147 and closed it via update_issue. Created new issue `[repo-assist] Monthly Activity 2026-07` with the carry-over Suggested Actions from June plus this run's two new PR-equivalents and two new "Check comment" items.

## Verification

- `dart format` → clean across all 11 changed files.
- `flutter analyze` on Task 5's 9 files → 1 pre-existing `prefer_const_constructors` info on `byok_dictionary_capability.dart:62` (`throw FormatException(...)`), unchanged.
- `flutter analyze` on Task 8's 2 files → No issues found!
- `flutter test test/features/ai/` → **57/57 pass** (8 new + 49 existing).
- `flutter test test/core/theme/dynamic_color/artwork_palette_test.dart` → **4/4 pass**.

## Backlog cursor

- `comments_made`: #8, #17, #23, #31, #39, #40, #41, #44, #45, #148, **#163 (new)**, **#151 (new)**.
- `prs_attempted`: 14 from previous runs plus **`repo-assist/refactor-worker-language-pair-2026-07-01` (commit 7204370)** and **`repo-assist/perf-palette-cache-invalidation-2026-07-01` (commit 6774b09)`** — both this run.
- `monthly_summary`: closed #147 (June) with wrap-up comment; created new July issue.

## Why

- For Task 5: Freshest actionable duplicate-code issue with a clear, surgical refactor recipe in the issue body. Centralising the inline pair removes the drift risk between the 7 call sites and gives the prompts/capabilities one source of truth.
- For Task 8: Item #2 on the perf-improver backlog — the smallest, safest-per-PR win explicitly called out in #151's June retrospective. The `palette_generator` isolate story (item #1) is blocked on a major dependency bump that needs explicit maintainer sign-off; this fix doesn't change any deps.
- For Task 2: Comments on #163 and #151 are substantive (not vague acknowledgements) — they describe the branch, the verification results, and the push recipe.
- For Task 11: Closing the June issue and opening July follows the workflow mandate. Carry-over items (PRs to push, comments to check) preserved verbatim so the maintainer doesn't lose the queue.
